summary: Cattle/customer management platform with admin and customer portals. Version 28 had working admin login. Current goal is to split the login page into two separate entry points — one for Admin and one for Customer — each with their own dedicated portal access.
architectural_notes: Version 28 is the stable baseline with working admin login — use it as reference for admin authentication flow
known_issues: Version 30 deployment is missing login credentials/access for both admin and customer portals

existing_features.title: Create a dedicated landing/login selection page that presents two clearly labeled entry points: 'Admin Login' and 'Customer Login', allowing users to choose their portal before proceeding.

Restore the working admin login flow from Version 28: the Admin login page must use Internet Identity authentication, verify admin role via the backend actor, and redirect to the admin dashboard on success. Display a clear error if the authenticated principal is not an admin.

Create a dedicated Customer Login page where customers authenticate using their username and password credentials managed in the backend CustomerAccount store. On success, store the customer session and redirect to the Customer Portal.

Update the app routing so that the root path ('/') renders the new login selection page. Protect the admin panel routes to redirect unauthenticated or non-admin users back to the admin login page. Protect the customer portal route to redirect unauthenticated customers back to the customer login page.

new_feature_requests.summary: Split login page into separate Admin and Customer entry points, restoring working admin login from Version 28
